perturbation.original_text: Without limiting the foregoing, where the Discloser is the Producer and a Recipient Representative is a cost consultant of the Client or Agency, the Recipient shall secure its Representative’s express written agreement that it will not make any business or other use of, and will in perpetuity hold in confidence, all Confidential Information acquired or learned by such Representative and shall not use or disclose such information except as permitted in this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Without limiting the foregoing, where the Discloser is the Producer and a Recipient Representative is a cost consultant of the Client or Agency, the Recipient shall secure its Representative’s express written agreement that it will hold in confidence all Confidential Information acquired or learned by such Representative and shall not use or disclose such information except as permitted in this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting 'that it will not make any business or other use of, and will in perpetuity hold in confidence, all Confidential Information acquired or learned by such Representative' , it weakens the protection afforded to the Producer's Confidential Information when disclosed to cost consultants. This creates an internal contradiction because, without the explicit prohibition against 'business or other use', a cost consultant might argue that using the information for internal analysis, without direct disclosure, is permissible, which contradicts the overall intent of confidentiality.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Non-Use and Non-Disclosure. Recipients will not disclose, distribute, copy or use any Confidential Information or the fact that a Transaction is being considered or is under a production contract without the prior written consent of the other Party and then only to the extent specified in such consent. Confidential Information may be used only to the extent reasonably required for the purposes of evaluating, producing or carrying out the Transaction; provided however, that Recipients may only disclose the Confidential Information to those of its contractors, vendors, directors, officers, advisors, agents and employees ("Representatives") who need to know such Confidential Information for the purpose of evaluating, performing or carrying out or producing the Transaction and who shall be bound by a written agreement or other legally enforceable written obligations to preserve the confidentiality of the Confidential Information in a manner consistent with this Agreement. The Recipient will be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by it or its Representatives, whether or not such Representatives are associated with the Discloser at the time of the breach. Without limiting the foregoing, where the Discloser is the Producer and a Recipient Representative is a cost consultant of the Client or Agency, the Recipient shall secure its Representative’s express written agreement that it will hold in confidence all Confidential Information acquired or learned by such Representative and shall not use or disclose such information except as permitted in this Agreement. Prohibited uses and disclosures by such Representative include, but are not limited to, uses for or disclosures to a competitor of the Producer and uses for or disclosures to any other client or prospective client of such Representative, regardless of whether the Confidential Information is aggregated with other information or its not identified with the Producer. Producer Confidential Information may be disclosed to the Owner (unless an independent third party is required by the FTC/DOJ) for purposes permitted under this Agreement and disclosed, on an aggregate basis, to other Agency Affiliates provided: (i) the Owner and each Agency Affiliate Recipient agrees to be bound by this Agreement; (ii) Producer Confidential Information to be disclosed is at least three months old; (iii) Producer Confidential Information is aggregated with similar types of confidential information of at least 4 other commercial producers with whom the Agency or Agency Affiliate have contracted to produce commercials and who have consented in writing to have their confidential information aggregated and disclosed in accordance with this procedure provided however that no aggregated producer confidential information represents more than 25% on a weighted basis of any statistic and further provided that all information is sufficiently aggregated such that it would not allow a Recipient to identify the Confidential Information of the Producer or of any other producer whose confidential information is aggregated.
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits the clause 'that it will not make any business or other use of, and will in perpetuity hold in confidence, all Confidential Information acquired or learned by such Representative'. The original text, specifically in the Contradicted Text, states 'Recipients will not disclose, distribute, copy or use any Confidential Information'. By removing the explicit prohibition against 'business or other use', the changed text creates a potential loophole where a cost consultant might argue that using the information for internal analysis, without direct disclosure, is permissible. This directly contradicts the overall intent, and the explicit requirements, of confidentiality as laid out in the original Contradicted Text, creating an in-text contradiction as defined by the Omissions - In Text Contradiction perturbation type.

perturbation.original_text: Producer Confidential Information may be disclosed to the Owner (unless an independent third party is required by the FTC/DOJ) for purposes permitted under this Agreement and disclosed, on an aggregate basis, to other Agency Affiliates provided: (i) the Owner and each Agency Affiliate Recipient agrees to be bound by this Agreement; (ii) Producer Confidential Information to be disclosed is at least three months old; (iii) Producer Confidential Information is aggregated with similar types of confidential information of at least 4 other commercial producers with whom the Agency or Agency Affiliate have contracted to produce commercials and who have consented in writing to have their confidential information aggregated and disclosed in accordance with this procedure provided however that no aggregated producer confidential information represents more than 25% on a weighted basis of any statistic and further provided that all information is sufficiently aggregated such that it would not allow a Recipient to identify the Confidential Information of the Producer or of any other producer whose confidential information is aggregated.
perturbation.changed_text: Producer Confidential Information may be disclosed to the Owner for purposes permitted under this Agreement and disclosed, on an aggregate basis, to other Agency Affiliates provided: (i) the Owner and each Agency Affiliate Recipient agrees to be bound by this Agreement; (ii) Producer Confidential Information to be disclosed is at least three months old; (iii) Producer Confidential Information is aggregated with similar types of confidential information of at least 4 other commercial producers with whom the Agency or Agency Affiliate have contracted to produce commercials and who have consented in writing to have their confidential information aggregated and disclosed in accordance with this procedure provided however that no aggregated producer confidential information represents more than 25% on a weighted basis of any statistic and further provided that all information is sufficiently aggregated such that it would not allow a Recipient to identify the Confidential Information of the Producer or of any other producer whose confidential information is aggregated.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase '(unless an independent third party is required by the FTC/DOJ)', it creates an in-text contradiction. The original clause acknowledges potential legal restrictions on disclosing confidential information without an independent third party, potentially due to antitrust concerns. Removing this acknowledgement implies that disclosure to the Owner is always permissible, which directly contradicts the potential legal restrictions that the omitted clause originally addressed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Non-Use and Non-Disclosure. Recipients will not disclose, distribute, copy or use any Confidential Information or the fact that a Transaction is being considered or is under a production contract without the prior written consent of the other Party and then only to the extent specified in such consent. Confidential Information may be used only to the extent reasonably required for the purposes of evaluating, producing or carrying out the Transaction; provided however, that Recipients may only disclose the Confidential Information to those of its contractors, vendors, directors, officers, advisors, agents and employees ("Representatives") who need to know such Confidential Information for the purpose of evaluating, performing or carrying out or producing the Transaction and who shall be bound by a written agreement or other legally enforceable written obligations to preserve the confidentiality of the Confidential Information in a manner consistent with this Agreement. The Recipient will be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by it or its Representatives, whether or not such Representatives are associated with the Discloser at the time of the breach. Without limiting the foregoing, where the Discloser is the Producer and a Recipient Representative is a cost consultant of the Client or Agency, the Recipient shall secure its Representative’s express written agreement that it will hold in confidence all Confidential Information acquired or learned by such Representative and shall not use or disclose such information except as permitted in this Agreement. Prohibited uses and disclosures by such Representative include, but are not limited to, uses for or disclosures to a competitor of the Producer and uses for or disclosures to any other client or prospective client of such Representative, regardless of whether the Confidential Information is aggregated with other information or its not identified with the Producer. Producer Confidential Information may be disclosed to the Owner for purposes permitted under this Agreement and disclosed, on an aggregate basis, to other Agency Affiliates provided: (i) the Owner and each Agency Affiliate Recipient agrees to be bound by this Agreement; (ii) Producer Confidential Information to be disclosed is at least three months old; (iii) Producer Confidential Information is aggregated with similar types of confidential information of at least 4 other commercial producers with whom the Agency or Agency Affiliate have contracted to produce commercials and who have consented in writing to have their confidential information aggregated and disclosed in accordance with this procedure provided however that no aggregated producer confidential information represents more than 25% on a weighted basis of any statistic and further provided that all information is sufficiently aggregated such that it would not allow a Recipient to identify the Confidential Information of the Producer or of any other producer whose confidential information is aggregated.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the phrase '(unless an independent third party is required by the FTC/DOJ)' from the original text. The original text acknowledged potential legal restrictions. Removing this acknowledgement now implies that disclosure to the Owner is always permissible. The contradicted text requires prior written consent, which is in direct conflict with the altered text because the altered text makes it seem as though prior written consent may not always be needed due to the phrase being removed, thus resulting in a strong, in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient shall reproduce Discloser's proprietary rights notices on any copies, in the same manner in which such notices were set forth in or on the original.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient shall reproduce Discloser's proprietary rights notices on any copies.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase 'in the same manner in which such notices were set forth in or on the original', the obligation to accurately reproduce proprietary rights notices becomes vague. This creates a potential contradiction because, without the explicit instruction to maintain the original format, a Recipient might argue that reproducing the essence of the notice is sufficient, even if the appearance is altered. This contradicts the Discloser's need to protect their proprietary rights with accurate and recognizable notices.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Maintenance of Confidentiality. Recipient agrees that it shall take reasonable measures to protect the secrecy of and avoid disclosure and unauthorized use of the Confidential Information. Without limiting the foregoing, Recipient shall take at least those measures that it takes to protect its own confidential information of a similar nature, but in no event less than reasonable measures. Recipient shall reproduce Discloser's proprietary rights notices on any copies. Recipient shall immediately notify Discloser upon discovery of any loss or unauthorized disclosure of any Confidential Information. If the Recipient receives a request to disclose all or any part of the Confidential Information under the terms of a valid subpoena or order issued by a court of competent jurisdiction or governmental body, Recipient agrees to (i) immediately notify the Discloser of the existence, terms and circumstances surrounding such a request, so that the Discloser may seek an appropriate protective order or other reliable written assurance that confidential treatment will be accorded to Confidential Information, and (ii) only disclose that portion of the Confidential Information which counsel advises is legally required to be disclosed. At all times, the Discloser shall retain sole ownership of its Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that the Recipient must reproduce the Discloser's proprietary rights notices 'in the same manner in which such notices were set forth in or on the original'. The changed text removes this requirement, only stating that the Recipient shall reproduce the notices, but omitting the 'same manner' clause. The contradicted text includes the statement 'Recipient shall reproduce Discloser's proprietary rights notices on any copies'. While the contradicted text already lacked the 'same manner' clause, the removal of that phrase from the original text, where it was present, creates a new and stronger contradiction because the explicit instruction on how to reproduce has been removed, which contrasts with the Discloser's intention to have their proprietary rights accurately reproduced, as implied in the Contradicted Text with the clause 'Maintenance of Confidentiality' which dictates that Recipient must protect the secrecy of and avoid unauthorized use of the Confidential Information. This makes enforcement unclear, creating a logical inconsistency.

perturbation.original_text: At the request of the Discloser, Recipient will promptly return to the Discloser or at Discloser’s instructions destroy any written or electronic Confidential Information and all physical media on which Confidential Information was received from the other Recipient, including any copies thereof, with a letter confirming that the Confidential Information has not been reproduced or copied or that all copies have been returned and that none of the Confidential Information was disclosed to anyone except in accordance with this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: At the request of the Discloser, Recipient will return to the Discloser or at Discloser’s instructions destroy any written or electronic Confidential Information and all physical media on which Confidential Information was received from the other Recipient, including any copies thereof.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'with a letter confirming that the Confidential Information has not been reproduced or copied or that all copies have been returned and that none of the Confidential Information was disclosed to anyone except in accordance with this Agreement', the requirement to provide written confirmation of compliance is eliminated. This creates an internal contradiction. Without this confirmation, the Discloser has no assurance that the Recipient has fully complied with the return/destruction obligation and that no unauthorized copies or disclosures persist, undercutting the overall goal of protecting confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Return of Materials. At the request of the Discloser, Recipient will return to the Discloser or at Discloser’s instructions destroy any written or electronic Confidential Information and all physical media on which Confidential Information was received from the other Recipient, including any copies thereof. The Recipient and its Representatives shall not retain any copies, extracts or other reproductions, in whole or in part, of such written or electronic material, except as may be needed to comply with applicable law and, provided that Recipient may retain one copy of Confidential Information in its confidential files for use only in the event a dispute arises between the Parties hereunder and only in connection with that dispute.
perturbation.justification: The original text required the Recipient to provide a letter confirming compliance with the return/destruction of Confidential Information, including assurance that no unauthorized copies or disclosures persisted. The changed text removes this requirement. The Contradicted Text states the Recipient shall not retain any copies, extracts or other reproductions of Confidential Information. Because the changed text removes the requirement to confirm this via a letter, it creates a contradiction. There is no longer a mechanism to enforce the 'shall not retain' obligation in the contradicted text as effectively as the original text did. Therefore, the changed text creates an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Parties expressly agree and acknowledge that any disputes relating to this Agreement will be exclusively adjudicated by federal and state courts located in_______________________.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties agree and acknowledge that any disputes relating to this Agreement will be adjudicated by courts.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'expressly' and 'exclusively' and 'federal and state courts located in_______________________.', the section about jurisdiction is less clear. This creates a contradiction because now it is not defined where those disagreements will be adujudicated, leaving any of the parties free to choose whatever court they feel is more conveniant, making the agreement less binding.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Miscellaneous. The terms of this Agreement may be modified or waived only by a separate writing signed by the Parties expressly so modifying or waiving such terms. No failure or delay by the Discloser in exercising any right, power or privilege shall operate as a waiver, nor shall any single or partial exercise preclude any other or further exercise of any right, power or privilege hereunder. The unenforceability of any provision of this Agreement will not affect the validity or enforceability of any other provision. This Agreement shall bind and inure to the benefit of the Parties hereto and their successors and assigns. This Agreement shall be governed by, and construed in accordance with, the laws of the State of ____________________ without giving effect to any conflicts or choice of laws provisions that would cause the application of the domestic substantive laws of any other jurisdiction. This Agreement contains the entire agreement between the Parties with respect to the subject matter hereof, and neither Party shall have any obligation, express or implied by law, with respect to trade secret or proprietary information of the other Party except as set forth herein. This Agreement may be executed in counterparts, with each an original and both of which together shall constitute one and the same instrument, and the Parties agree that they will be bound by electronic signatures. The Parties agree and acknowledge that any disputes relating to this Agreement will be adjudicated by courts. Each person who signs this Agreement represents that he/she has the authority and is empowered to sign and deliver this Agreement on behalf of the Recipient that he/she represents.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that disputes will be adjudicated in federal and state courts located in a specific location. The changed text removes the words 'expressly' and 'exclusively' and the location, making it less clear where disputes will be adjudicated. The contradicted text states 'The Parties agree and acknowledge that any disputes relating to this Agreement will be adjudicated by courts.'. The original text defined the specific courts, while the changed text broadens this, creating a contradiction. The removal of the location and exclusivity introduces ambiguity, violating the principle of the agreement. This adheres to the definition of Omissions - In Text Contradiction.
